steps.text: Make the roux: In a heavy pot over medium heat, whisk oil and flour together. Cook, whisking constantly, until the roux turns a deep chocolate-brown (do not walk away). If it smells burnt or shows black specks, start over.
Add the holy trinity: Stir in onion, bell pepper, and celery. Cook until softened, then add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds more.
Brown the sausage and chicken: Add andouille and cook until lightly browned. Add chicken and stir to coat in the roux mixture; cook until the chicken loses its raw color on the outside.
Build the gumbo: Slowly add chicken stock while stirring to prevent lumps. Add bay leaves, Cajun seasoning, thyme, paprika, black pepper, and salt.
Simmer: Bring to a gentle simmer. Cook uncovered until the gumbo thickens and the chicken is tender, stirring occasionally to keep the bottom from sticking.
Add okra: Stir in okra and simmer until tender and the gumbo is glossy and cohesive.
Finish: Remove bay leaves. Stir in green onions and parsley. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Serve: Ladle gumbo over rice. Serve hot sauce on the side.
